summary: Matched and cleaned up HSD_TExpDag scheduling/simplification code in sysdolphin baselib. The PR changed texpdag.c and texpdag.h, replacing bulky decompiler-shaped control flow with clearer bitset dependency helpers, typed u32 dependency matrices, local static GX argument tables, and reuse of IsThroughColor/IsThroughAlpha predicates in HSD_TExpSimplify2. Header prototypes were updated to match the improved u32 bitmask signatures. No substantive review feedback was present in the slice.
what_changed: Added an include of "texp.h" to texpdag.c, enabling direct use of TExp helpers/macros such as IsThroughColor and IsThroughAlpha.
Changed order_dag dependency parameters from int* to u32* and renamed scheduling state parameters from node/scheduled/available/best_score/best_order to idx/done_set/ready_set/min/min_order.
Introduced a static make_dependancy_mtx helper that builds per-node dependency bitmasks from HSD_TExpDag::depend entries.
Rewrote make_full_dependancy_mtx to operate on u32 bitmasks and compute the transitive dependency closure with straightforward nested loops and a bool changed flag.
Refactored HSD_TExpSchedule to use dep_mtx, full_dep_matrix, order, and min_order arrays, call make_dependancy_mtx, and iterate over result[i] instead of pointer/count temporaries.
Moved the GX color/alpha argument lookup tables into HSD_TExpSchedule as local static arrays named c_in, a_in, and args, replacing file-scope HSD_TExpDag_80407AA0_* arrays.
Simplified HSD_TExpSimplify2 by replacing manually expanded through-color and through-alpha condition chains with IsThroughColor(src_exp) and IsThroughAlpha(src_exp).
Updated texpdag.h prototypes for order_dag and make_full_dependancy_mtx to use u32* dependency matrices and u32 scheduling bitsets.
smart_moves: Recognized dependency matrices as bitsets and changed their type from int* to u32*, making the code’s intent match the 1 << idx operations.
Factored dependency-matrix construction out of HSD_TExpSchedule into make_dependancy_mtx, reducing clutter in the matched scheduling function.
Kept the recursive order_dag search structure but improved semantic names: done_set and ready_set document the bitmask state better than scheduled and available.
Used local static GX argument tables in HSD_TExpSchedule, which preserved lookup-table behavior while removing opaque autogenerated global names.
Reused existing semantic predicates IsThroughColor and IsThroughAlpha instead of retaining chains of temporary r3/r4/r5/r6 flags, improving readability without broad behavioral changes.
Preserved the explicit fallthrough behavior in HSD_TExpSimplify2 for HSD_TE_KONST to HSD_TE_IMM cases while cleaning up variable names and references.
decomp_lessons: When a matrix is manipulated exclusively with bit operations like 1 << idx and OR closures, prefer u32 arrays over int arrays; this can both clarify semantics and help matching.
Opaque BSS/data names for small lookup tables can often become local static arrays once their use is understood, especially for GX enum remapping tables.
Decompiler-generated boolean ladders can sometimes be replaced by already-known helper predicates, as shown by IsThroughColor and IsThroughAlpha in HSD_TExpSimplify2.

For scheduling/search routines, semantic parameter names that describe the bitset role are valuable: idx, done_set, ready_set, min, and min_order communicate the algorithm better than generic node or best_score names.
Extracting small helper functions such as make_dependancy_mtx can make the caller match while also exposing a reusable conceptual unit: direct dependency bitmask construction.
Transitive closure over dependency bitsets can be expressed cleanly with a changed loop and nested bit propagation, avoiding hand-unrolled copy/setup code when the compiler still matches.
naming_conventions: Use dep_mtx for the direct dependency matrix bitset.
Use full_dep_matrix for the transitive dependency matrix.
Use done_set and ready_set for recursive scheduling bitsets.
Use min and min_order for the current best scheduling score/order when the objective is to minimize register pressure.
Use src_exp and src_sel for source expression/selector temporaries in simplification code.
Use c_in, a_in, and args for local GX argument remapping tables rather than keeping address-derived names such as HSD_TExpDag_80407AA0_44.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed prototypes and locals from int dependency arrays to u32 dependency arrays to better model bitmask operations and remove casts in assign_reg/order_dag calls.
Replaced a large manual copy/unroll section in make_full_dependancy_mtx with a simple for-loop copy from dep to full, followed by an iterative closure loop.
Kept order_dag’s recursive branch behavior, including the special case for dag->nb_dep == 1 and dag->depend[0]->idx, while cleaning up variable naming and types.
Used casts when passing u32 order and min_order arrays to order_dag’s int* order parameters, indicating that only some arrays were converted to u32 while the public order parameter remained int*.
Moved previously file-scope static arrays into function-local static arrays inside HSD_TExpSchedule, which can affect data placement/naming while preserving constant table use.
Replaced temporary condition flags in HSD_TExpSimplify2 with calls to IsThroughColor and IsThroughAlpha, a semantic-matching tactic that depends on including texp.h.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided slice.
The PR had one review and one issue comment according to counts, but their bodies were not present in the excerpt, so reviewer intent cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching HSD_TExpSchedule or order_dag to see whether u32 order arrays or int* casts were further cleaned up.
Search for other HSD_TExp simplification code that can reuse IsThroughColor or IsThroughAlpha instead of expanded condition chains.
Check whether make_dependancy_mtx remained static in texpdag.c or was later exposed/renamed to fix the misspelling 'dependancy'.
Search for remaining HSD_TExpDag_80407AA0_* symbols or address-derived lookup table names in baselib.
